title: The One With the Candy Hearts

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: She barely knows me. We just live in the same building.
Chandler: Any contact?
Ross: She lent me an egg once.
Joey: **You're in!**
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 작은 접점이라도 있으니 기회가 생겼다 — 이미 관계의 문이 열렸다는 뜻
expressions.nuance: 달걀 한 개라는 극히 사소한 접점도 '입구'로 해석하며 상대를 격려하는 구어 표현. 연애뿐 아니라 비즈니스·취업 맥락에서도 '발을 들여놨어'의 의미로 자주 씀.
expressions.example: She texted you first? You're in! / 그녀가 먼저 문자 보냈다고? 됐다, 기회 잡은 거야!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Alright Ross, look. You're feeling a lot of pain right now. You're angry. You're hurting. Can I tell you what the answer is?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Monica- Hi! Um, Monica, you're scaring me. I mean, you're like, you're like all chaotic and twirly. And not-not in a good way.

occ.speaker: Joanne
occ.text: Look at you in the apron. You look like you're in a play.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Come on, Ross, you gotta **get back in the game** here, ok? The Rachel thing's not happening, your ex-wife is a lesbian — I don't think we need a third...
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 이별이나 실패 후 연애(또는 경쟁)에 다시 적극적으로 뛰어들다
expressions.nuance: 연애 활동 전체를 스포츠처럼 비유한 표현. 한동안 연애를 쉬거나 실연 당한 사람에게 다시 시작하라고 촉구할 때 씀.
expressions.example: You've been moping around for months. It's time to get back in the game. / 몇 달을 풀이 죽어 있었잖아. 이제 다시 시작할 때야.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I've gotta get back in the game.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: And y'know what, I just realised, in the last year I've only gone out with two guys, Richard and Julio. You gotta help me out here, you gotta set me up, you gotta get me back in the game.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: Oh, uh, listen, about tonight...
Joey: No, no, no, don't you dare **bail on me**. The only reason she's goin' out with me is because I said I could bring a friend for her friend.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 약속이나 계획을 갑자기 취소해 상대방을 실망시키다
expressions.nuance: 상대방이 나를 믿고 기대하고 있을 때 발을 빼는 것. 'don't bail on me'는 강하게 약속 이행을 요구하는 표현으로, 직역하면 '나한테서 도망가지 마'.
expressions.example: Everyone bailed on the camping trip at the last minute, so I ended up going alone. / 다들 마지막 순간에 캠핑을 취소해버려서 결국 혼자 가게 됐어.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Y'know what, y'know what, I'm-I'm not the one that wanted that, that break, okay. You're the one that bailed on us. You're the one that, that ran when things got just a little rough!

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: (angrily) You **set me up with** the woman that I've dumped twice in the last five months!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 누군가를 다른 사람과 소개팅시켜 주다
expressions.nuance: 제3자가 두 사람의 만남을 주선하는 상황에서 씀. 'set up' 단독으로도 같은 의미가 되며, 'blind date' 주선에서 자주 등장.
expressions.example: My coworker set me up with her friend, but we had absolutely nothing in common. / 동료가 친구랑 소개팅시켜줬는데 공통점이 하나도 없었어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No! Uh, I d'know! The point is, if you were gonna set me up with someone, I'd like to think you'd set me up with someone like him.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: All right! Fine! But it's just a lunch date, no more than an hour! And from now on I get my own dates, I don't want you setting me up with anybody ever again!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Umm, my friend, Bonnie. She just always thought Ross was really cute, and now that you two aren't together, she asked if I could set it up, but if you're not cool with it...

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: You set me up with the woman that I've **dumped** twice in the last five months!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 연인을 일방적으로 차다, 이별을 먼저 통보하다
expressions.nuance: 이별을 통보하는 쪽이 'dump'를 하는 것. 직접적이고 캐주얼한 표현으로, 일방적인 이별을 강조하며 당한 쪽 입장에서는 상처가 느껴지는 단어.
expressions.example: She dumped him right before the holidays. That's cold. / 그녀가 연휴 직전에 그를 찼어. 진짜 심하다.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: So, got any advice? Y'know, as someone who's recently been- dumped?

occ.speaker: Paula
occ.text: Honey.. you should always feel the thing. Listen, if that's how you feel about the guy, Monica, dump him!

occ.speaker: Kiki
occ.text: Well, the biggest news is still you dumping Barry at the altar!

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: She said yes.
Chandler: Yes! **Way to go**, man! (Chandler and Ross hug. Something crunches in Ross' shirt pocket.) Still got the egg, huh?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 잘했어!, 해냈네! — 즉각적인 축하와 칭찬의 감탄사
expressions.nuance: 누군가가 어떤 일을 성공했을 때 터져 나오는 자연스러운 격려. 진심 어린 칭찬으로도, 가끔 'Way to go, genius…'처럼 비꼬는 말로도 씀.
expressions.example: You finished the marathon in under four hours? Way to go! / 4시간 안에 마라톤 완주했다고? 대단한데!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: So, you gals wanna hand over your money now? That way, we don't have to go through the formality of actually playing.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh, uh, ok. Right this way. All the other pregnant women seem to be goin' in here.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Well, then, I think, I think the guy is scum. I hate him. I mean I actually-I-I physically hate him. I always have. You are way too good to be with a guy like that.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Here's my credit card. **Dinner's on me**. I'm sorry, Chandler.
Chandler: I hope she throws up on you.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 내가 낼게, 내가 쏠게 — 비용을 내가 부담한다
expressions.nuance: 식사·음료의 비용을 자신이 내겠다는 표현. 'This round's on me'(이번 잔은 내가), 'It's on the house'(가게가 서비스로 주는 것) 등 변형이 많음.
expressions.example: You stayed late to help me finish this. Lunch is on me tomorrow. / 늦게까지 도와줬잖아. 내일 점심은 내가 살게.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: Ok, I'm **makin' a break for it**, I'm goin' out the window.
Joey: No, no, no, don't! I've been waitin' for like, forever to go out with Lorraine.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 싫은 상황에서 재빨리 도망치다, 탈출을 시도하다
expressions.nuance: 실제 탈출뿐 아니라 불편한 모임이나 어색한 자리를 빠져나가려 할 때 비유적으로 씀. 약간 유머러스하고 드라마틱한 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: The meeting dragged on for three hours, so I made a break for it during the coffee break. / 회의가 3시간째 이어지자 커피 브레이크 틈을 타 슬쩍 빠져나왔어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh my god. Oh my god. (She decides to make a break for it.) Excuse me. Move! Move! Emergency! Excuse me! (She tries climbing over a bench and falls down.)

expressions.dialogue:
Kristin: You mean they're lovers.
Ross: If you wanna **put a label on it**.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 관계나 상황을 특정 단어로 딱 규정하다, 이름을 붙이다
expressions.nuance: 관계를 명확히 정의하기를 꺼리거나 애매하게 남겨두고 싶을 때 씀. 'I don't want to put a label on us yet'처럼 부정문으로 더 자주 등장.
expressions.example: We've been seeing each other for months, but neither of us wants to put a label on it. / 몇 달째 만나고 있는데, 둘 다 관계를 딱 규정하기는 싫어.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: But what do you say we just put that aside for now, you know? Let's just **stick a pin in it**, ok? Because, we're great together, you know.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 논의나 사안을 일단 보류해두다, 나중에 다시 다루기 위해 잠깐 제쳐두다
expressions.nuance: 회의나 대화에서 특정 주제를 나중에 다시 꺼내기 위해 잠시 멈출 때 씀. 'table it'과 비슷하지만 더 구어적이며 마치 메모지에 핀을 꽂아두는 이미지.
expressions.example: That's a great point, but let's stick a pin in it and circle back after the presentation. / 좋은 지적인데, 일단 보류해두고 발표 끝나고 다시 얘기하자.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: You know, this is still pretty hot. (He picks his head up.) You know the whole '**getting on with your life**' thing. Well, do I have to?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 힘든 일을 뒤로 하고 일상과 미래로 나아가다, 앞으로 계속 살아가다
expressions.nuance: 이별, 상실, 실패 후 일상으로 돌아가 앞으로 나아가는 것. 조언('You need to get on with your life')이나 이 대화처럼 저항('Do I have to?')의 맥락에서 씀.
expressions.example: I know losing the job hurt, but you have to get on with your life and start applying again. / 실직이 힘들었던 거 알지만, 앞으로 나아가서 다시 지원해야 해.

expressions.dialogue: Janice: Why do you think we keep **ending up together**? New Year's? Who invited who? Valentine's? Who asked who into whose bed?
expressions.speaker: Janice
expressions.meaning: 결국 (다시) 함께하게 되다 — 특히 헤어지고 만나기를 반복하는 패턴
expressions.nuance: 'end up'은 예상치 못했거나 반복되는 결과를 나타낼 때 씀. 연애에서는 '결국 다시 사귀게 되다'의 뉘앙스이며, 아이러니나 운명처럼 묘사할 때 잘 어울림.
expressions.example: No matter how many times they broke up, they always ended up together. / 몇 번을 헤어지든 그들은 결국 항상 다시 함께했다.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Ok, then you're gonna have to understand that you're with a guy who's not gonna stop planning his future with you because he knows that we're gonna end up together and if that scares you, tough, 'cause you're gonna have to deal with that.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Because, I-I should've called! Y'know if I had just called her after our big, stupid fight, she never would've gone out with Nick, and they would've ended up in bed together. I threw her at his man nipples!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Yeah, we ended up spending the day together and had such a great time!

expressions.dialogue: Janice: You **seek me out**. Something deep in your soul calls out to me like a foghorn. Janice, Janice. You want me. You need me. You can't live without me.
expressions.speaker: Janice
expressions.meaning: 의식적이든 무의식적이든 적극적으로 누군가를 찾아내다, 찾아가다
expressions.nuance: 단순히 만나는 것이 아니라 능동적으로 상대를 찾아가는 행위를 강조. 강한 끌림이나 필요에 의해 움직인다는 뉘앙스이며 감정적·철학적 맥락에서도 씀.
expressions.example: Whenever she's stressed, she seeks out her college friends for support. / 스트레스받을 때마다 그녀는 대학 친구들을 찾아간다.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Oh great now you're seeking me out to make jokes? I mean I can see for all hanging out but to come to my home!

expressions.expression: things aren't going to work out
expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Ok, Janice. Look, there's no way for me to tell you this. At least there's no new way for me to tell you this. I just don't think things are gonna **work out**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: (관계나 상황이) 잘 풀리지 않을 것 같다 — 완곡한 이별 통보
expressions.nuance: 직접적인 이별 표현('I don't love you')을 피하며 완곡하게 쓰는 표현. 'work out'은 '좋은 결과로 이어지다'의 뜻으로, 연애뿐 아니라 사업·계획에도 씀.
expressions.example: I really tried, but I don't think this business partnership is going to work out. / 정말 노력했지만, 이 사업 파트너십은 잘 안 될 것 같아.

expressions.expression: slather something on / slather one's body with something
expressions.dialogue: Joey: She said she wants to **slather my body with stuff** and then lick it off. I'm not even sure what slathering is, but I definitely want to be a part of it.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 무언가를 몸이나 표면에 아낌없이 듬뿍 두껍게 바르다
expressions.nuance: 음식, 크림, 소스 등을 넉넉하게 바르는 생생한 구어 표현. 선크림, 잼, 버터 등 일상에서도 자연스럽게 쓰며 과장의 뉘앙스를 담기도 함.
expressions.example: She slathered sunscreen on her arms before heading to the beach. / 그녀는 해변에 가기 전에 팔에 선크림을 듬뿍 발랐다.

expressions.dialogue:
Lorraine: Uh, can we have three chocolate mousses to go please?
Joey: **I'm outta here**. Here's my credit card. Dinner's on me. I'm sorry, Chandler.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 나 간다, 여기서 빠진다 — 즉각적이고 단호한 이탈 선언
expressions.nuance: 'out of here'의 구어 축약형. 결정적인 순간에 단호하게 떠남을 선언할 때 씀. 흥분, 설렘, 도망 등 다양한 감정과 함께 쓰임.
expressions.example: This party is dead. I'm outta here. / 이 파티 완전 별로야. 나 간다.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Right.  Thanks.  It's June.  I'm outta here. (Exits.)

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (leaving) I'm outta here.

occ.speaker: Eddie
occ.text: Well you guys, I'm outta here. See ya pals.

expressions.dialogue: [대본 지문: Ross is eyeing a beautiful woman at the counter, and Joey and Chandler are egging him on to go talk to her. **No pun intended**. I mean it.]
expressions.speaker: 대본 작가 (무대 지문)
expressions.meaning: 방금 한 말에 (우연히) 말장난이 들어 있지만 의도한 건 아니라는 뜻 — 이미 pun이 있음을 역설적으로 인정하는 셈
expressions.nuance: 'egg him on'에 'egg'(달걀)이 들어 있어 달걀 에피소드와 겹치는 말장난이 생겼음을 인정한 것. 대화 중 우연히 pun이 생겼을 때 웃음을 유발하며 씀.
expressions.example: We really need to iron out the details — no pun intended — before the laundry startup launches. / 런칭 전에 세부사항을 다림질해야 해요 — 말장난은 아니지만요. (iron out = 해결하다)

expressions.expression: What do you say (we) ~? / What's say ~?
expressions.dialogue: Ross: Here's a wacky thought. Um, **what's say** you and I give it another shot? No no no, I know what you're gonna say, you're a lesbian. But what do you say we just put that aside for now...
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: ~하는 거 어때? — 상대의 동의를 구하며 제안할 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 부드럽고 캐주얼한 제안 방식으로 'How about we ~?'와 비슷하나 더 구어적. 'What say we grab lunch?'처럼 일상 대화에서 자연스럽게 씀.
expressions.example: What do you say we take the afternoon off and catch a movie? / 오후에 퇴근하고 영화 보러 가는 거 어때?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: So what're you saying here? I should shave again, pick up some wine, what?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: What'd I say?

occ.speaker: Coma Guy
occ.text: Well, what do you want me to say?

expressions.dialogue: Ross: What's say you and I **give it another shot**? No no no, I know what you're gonna say, you're a lesbian. But what do you say we just put that aside for now...
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 다시 한 번 시도해보다
expressions.nuance: 'shot'은 '기회, 시도'의 뜻. 'try again'보다 더 캐주얼하고 자연스러운 구어 표현. 연애, 일, 협상 등 다양한 맥락에서 씀.
expressions.example: The first interview was rough, but they offered to give me another shot next month. / 첫 면접이 잘 안 됐는데 다음 달에 한 번 더 기회를 주겠다고 했어.

expressions.expression: I'll take X over Y any day
expressions.dialogue: Monica: Well, I'll **take** a little crying **any day over** Howard-the-'I-win'-guy. 'I win! I win!' I went out with the guy for two months — I didn't get to win once.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: Y보다 X가 훨씬 낫다, 어떤 상황에서도 X를 선택하겠다
expressions.nuance: 두 가지 선택지 중 하나를 강하게 선호할 때 씀. 'any day'는 '언제라도, 무조건'의 강조 표현으로 비교 우위를 과장해서 드러냄.
expressions.example: I'll take an honest critic over empty praise any day. / 공허한 칭찬보다는 솔직한 비판이 언제라도 낫지.

expressions.dialogue: [대본 지문: Ross is eyeing a beautiful woman at the counter, and Joey and Chandler are **egging him on** to go talk to her.]
expressions.speaker: 대본 작가 (무대 지문)
expressions.meaning: 누군가를 말이나 행동으로 부추기다, 충동질하다
expressions.nuance: 상대방이 망설이거나 꺼리는 행동을 하도록 계속 자극할 때 씀. 중립적이거나 약간 부정적인 뉘앙스이지만 친구 사이에서는 장난스럽게도 씀.
expressions.example: He probably wouldn't have said anything if his friends hadn't egged him on. / 친구들이 부추기지 않았다면 그는 아무 말도 안 했을 거야.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Well, I mean, I'm not my sister's, you know, whatever, and um... I mean, it's true, we were one egg, once, but err, you know, we've grown apart, so, um... I don't know, why not? Okay.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh come on. Nobody likes two different kinds of eggs equally. You like one better than the other and I wanna know which.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: These people'll **scooch** down. You guys'll **scooch**, won't you? Let's try **scooching**! Come on. Come on.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 좁은 자리에서 옆이나 아래로 살짝 이동하다, 비켜 앉다
expressions.nuance: 'scoot'의 구어 변형으로 매우 친근하고 캐주얼한 표현. 영화관, 식당, 버스 등 자리를 나눠야 할 때 자연스럽게 씀.
expressions.example: Can you scooch over a little? I need to squeeze in here. / 조금만 옆으로 이동해 줄래? 여기 비집고 들어가야 해서.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: She'll love it. Go with the egg, my friend.
Joey: Think it'll work?
Chandler: No, **it's suicide**. The man's got an egg.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 스스로를 망하게 만드는 끔찍한 실수이다 — 자멸 행위다 (비유)
expressions.nuance: 실제 자살이 아닌, 특정 행동이 자멸로 이어진다는 과장 표현. 'career suicide', 'social suicide' 등 앞에 명사를 붙이면 더 구체적인 의미로 씀.
expressions.example: Arguing with the CEO at your very first company dinner? That's career suicide. / 첫 회사 만찬에서 CEO와 언쟁을? 그건 커리어 자살행위야.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Ah, it's career stuff. I don't know if you heard but they killed off my character on the show.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: (entering from bathroom, with an issue of Cosmo) All right, I took the quiz, and it turns out, I do put career before men. (to Joey) Get up.

occ.speaker: Frank
occ.text: Yeah, y'know when we found out we were going to have a baby, y'know I figured y'know like I should y'know have like a career-and I love refrigerators!

expressions.dialogue: Janice: You seek me out. Something deep in your soul **calls out to me** like a foghorn. Janice, Janice. You want me. You need me. You can't live without me.
expressions.speaker: Janice
expressions.meaning: 마음 깊은 곳에서 누군가나 무언가에 강하게 끌리다, 내면이 부르다
expressions.nuance: 의식적이 아닌 본능적·감정적 끌림을 묘사할 때 씀. 저항할 수 없는 이끌림이나 소명감을 시적으로 표현하는 것으로, 'foghorn'(안개 속 뱃고동) 비유와 함께 강도를 높임.
expressions.example: Every time I walk past a bookstore, something calls out to me and I can't leave empty-handed. / 서점 앞을 지날 때마다 무언가가 나를 부르는 것 같아서 빈손으로 나올 수가 없어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: No! (Pause) But, they're callin' out to me! I mean this little guy (Holds up a small one) even crawled up into my lap. Oh come on, Chandler wouldn't mind if I opened just one present! What do you think it is?

expressions.expression: You can't do this to me
expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Uh, we might be leaving now.
Chandler: Tell me it's 'you and me' we.
Joey: She said she wants to slather my body with stuff...
Chandler: Ok, you can **not** do this to me.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 나한테 이럴 수 없어, 이러면 안 되지 — 배신감이나 억울함을 담은 항의
expressions.nuance: 친한 사이에서 상대가 나를 곤란하게 만들거나 배신할 때 쓰는 표현. 진심 어린 호소부터 유머러스한 불평까지 폭넓게 씀. 'not'에 강세를 주면 더 강조됨.
expressions.example: You're the only one who knows the password and you're going on vacation tomorrow? You can't do this to me! / 비밀번호 아는 사람이 너뿐인데 내일 휴가 간다고? 나한테 이러면 안 되지!
